name: p0-geyser-leak-ceiling-stain
description: There's a brown water stain on the ceiling directly below where the geyser sits in the roof space. The stain has been growing over the past two days. I cannot safely get up into the roof to inspect the geyser. Photo attached showing the stain spreading on the ceiling.

hypotheses.label: Geyser tank or pressure-relief valve leaking (hot water)
evidence_for: Brown growing stain directly below geyser location is the classic signature of a slow hot-side leak
Two-day progression matches developing tank corrosion or a stuck-open PRV
evidence_against: Cannot confirm without seeing the geyser itself

hypotheses.label: Cold-side inlet leak above the geyser (flexi or isolating valve)
evidence_for: Cold inlet failures are common and produce an identical ceiling stain
Often presents through an overflowing drip tray
evidence_against: Cannot determine which fitting is leaking without roof access

mockAgentOutput.what_we_dont_know: Whether the source is the hot-water side (geyser tank or PRV) or the cold-water inlet plumbing above it. The user cannot safely enter the roof to inspect.
mockAgentOutput.why_it_matters: Both faults require an urgent plumber visit, but the contractor's first action and parts list differ — hot-side faults usually mean a geyser replacement; cold-side leaks are often a single fitting.

chips.text: I touched the wet ceiling and the water feels warm.
I touched the wet ceiling and the water feels cold.
I switched the geyser off at the DB and the stain stopped growing within hours.

committed_observations: brown water stain on ceiling below geyser
explicit_unknowns: exact leak source (tank, drip tray, or pressure-relief valve)

failure_baseline_today.observed_behaviour: System loops in clarification asking for a clearer photo of the geyser — which the user has explicitly said they cannot safely produce. No urgency framing in the response.
failure_baseline_today.why_it_fails: The system can't recognise 'further clarification would yield no actionable signal' and falls back to 'ask for a clearer photo'. There is no rubric for hazard escalation: when a leak is actively growing, the right move is to commit at low confidence with urgency, not to keep asking.

expected.phase_6_commit_low_confidence_rationale: Top hypothesis confidence_alone=0.7 (<0.75) and gap to second=0.2 (<0.25). Even though the touch-test chips discriminate, the broader hazard signal (active growing leak, user unable to investigate safely) promotes the recommendation to commit_low_confidence with urgency rather than ask another round. Phase 6 must include a hazard-override rule alongside the pure-logic completion criteria, OR Phase 7 prose conditioning must escalate urgency on commit_low_confidence outputs for active-damage cases.
expected.phase6_action_note: Phase 6 produces ask because chips discriminate. Phase 7 prose conditioning overlays hazard escalation and switches the surfaced action to commit_low_confidence with urgency framing.
